NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Contemplation of David when Doeg the Edomite went and told Saul, and said to him, “David has gone to the house of Ahimelech.” Why do you boast in evil, O mighty man? The goodness of God endures continually.
NKJV.2: Your tongue devises destruction, Like a sharp razor, working deceitfully.
NKJV.3: You love evil more than good, Lying rather than speaking righteousness. Selah
NKJV.4: You love all devouring words, You deceitful tongue.
NKJV.5: God shall likewise destroy you forever; He shall take you away, and pluck you out of your dwelling place, And uproot you from the land of the living. Selah
NKJV.6: The righteous also shall see and fear, And shall laugh at him, saying,
NKJV.7: “Here is the man who did not make God his strength, But trusted in the abundance of his riches, And strengthened himself in his wickedness.”
NKJV.8: But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God; I trust in the mercy of God forever and ever.
NKJV.9: I will praise You forever, Because You have done it; And in the presence of Your saints I will wait on Your name, for it is good.

ESV.1: Why do you boast of evil, O mighty man? The steadfast love of God endures all the day.
ESV.2: Your tongue plots destruction, like a sharp razor, you worker of deceit.
ESV.3: You love evil more than good, and lying more than speaking what is right. Selah
ESV.4: You love all words that devour, O deceitful tongue.
ESV.5: But God will break you down forever; he will snatch and tear you from your tent; he will uproot you from the land of the living. Selah
ESV.6: The righteous shall see and fear, and shall laugh at him, saying,
ESV.7: “See the man who would not make God his refuge, but trusted in the abundance of his riches and sought refuge in his own destruction!”
ESV.8: But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God. I trust in the steadfast love of God forever and ever.
ESV.9: I will thank you forever, because you have done it. I will wait for your name, for it is good, in the presence of the godly.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Maskil of David, when Doeg the Edomite came and told Saul and said to him, 'David has come to the house of Ahimelech.' Why do you boast in evil, O mighty man? The lovingkindness of God [endures] all day long.
NASB.2: Your tongue devises destruction, Like a sharp razor, O worker of deceit.
NASB.3: You love evil more than good, Falsehood more than speaking what is right. Selah.
NASB.4: You love all words that devour, O deceitful tongue.
NASB.5: But God will break you down forever; He will snatch you up and tear you away from [your] tent, And uproot you from the land of the living. Selah.
NASB.6: The righteous will see and fear, And will laugh at him, [saying],
NASB.7: 'Behold, the man who would not make God his refuge, But trusted in the abundance of his riches [And] was strong in his [evil] desire.'
NASB.8: But as for me, I am like a green olive tree in the house of God; I trust in the lovingkindness of God forever and ever.
NASB.9: I will give You thanks forever, because You have done [it], And I will wait on Your name, for [it is] good, in the presence of Your godly ones.

NLT.1:
Why do you boast about your crimes, great warrior?
Don’t you realize God’s justice continues forever?
NLT.2:
All day long you plot destruction.
Your tongue cuts like a sharp razor;
you’re an expert at telling lies.
NLT.3:
You love evil more than good
and lies more than truth. Interlude
NLT.4:
You love to destroy others with your words,
you liar!
NLT.5:
But God will strike you down once and for all.
He will pull you from your home
and uproot you from the land of the living. Interlude
NLT.6:
The righteous will see it and be amazed.
They will laugh and say,
NLT.7:
“Look what happens to mighty warriors
who do not trust in God.
They trust their wealth instead
and grow more and more bold in their wickedness.”
NLT.8:
But I am like an olive tree, thriving in the house of God.
I will always trust in God’s unfailing love.
NLT.9:
I will praise you forever, O God,
for what you have done.
I will trust in your good name
in the presence of your faithful people.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A contemplation by David, when Doeg the Edomite came and told Saul, “David has come to Ahimelech’s house.” Why do you boast of mischief, mighty man? God’s loving kindness endures continually.
WEB.2: Your tongue plots destruction, like a sharp razor, working deceitfully.
WEB.3: You love evil more than good, lying rather than speaking the truth. Selah.
WEB.4: You love all devouring words, you deceitful tongue.
WEB.5: God will likewise destroy you forever. He will take you up, and pluck you out of your tent, and root you out of the land of the living. Selah.
WEB.6: The righteous also will see it, and fear, and laugh at him, saying,
WEB.7: “Behold, this is the man who didn’t make God his strength, but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.”
WEB.8: But as for me, I am like a green olive tree in God’s house. I trust in God’s loving kindness forever and ever.
WEB.9: I will give you thanks forever, because you have done it. I will hope in your name, for it is good, in the presence of your saints.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A of David. When Doeg the Edomite went and reported to Saul, telling him, “David went to Ahimelech’s house.” ⓖ [1] Why boast about evil, you hero! ⓗ
God’s faithful love is constant. ⓘ
CSB17.2:
Like a sharpened razor,
your tongue devises destruction,
working treachery. ⓙ
CSB17.3:
You love evil instead of good,
lying instead of speaking truthfully. ⓚ Selah
CSB17.4:
You love any words that destroy,
you treacherous tongue! ⓛ
CSB17.5:
This is why God will bring you down forever.
He will take you, ripping you out of your tent;
he will uproot you from the land of the living. ⓜ Selah
CSB17.6:
The righteous will see and fear,
and they will derisively say about that hero, [1] ⓝ
CSB17.7:
“Here is the man
who would not make God his refuge,
but trusted in the abundance of his riches,
taking refuge in his destructive behavior.” ⓐ
CSB17.8:
But I am like a flourishing olive tree
in the house of God; ⓑ
I trust in God’s faithful love forever and ever. ⓒ
CSB17.9:
I will praise you forever for what you have done.
In the presence of your faithful people,
I will put my hope in your name, for it is good. ⓓ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A skillful song, or a didactic or reflective poem. A Psalm of David, when Doeg the Edomite came and told Saul, "David has come to the house of Ahimelech." Why do you boast of evil, O mighty man?
The lovingkindness of God endures all day long.
AMP.2:
Your tongue devises destruction,
Like a sharp razor, working deceitfully.
AMP.3:
You love evil more than good,
And falsehood more than speaking what is right. Selah.
AMP.4:
You love all words that devour,
O deceitful tongue.
AMP.5:
But God will break you down forever;
He will take you away and tear you away from your tent,
And uproot you from the land of the living. Selah.
AMP.6:
The righteous will see it and fear,
And will [scoffingly] laugh, saying,
AMP.7:
"Look, [this is] the man who would not make God his strength [his stronghold and fortress],
But trusted in the abundance of his riches,
Taking refuge in his wealth."
AMP.8:
But as for me, I am like a green olive tree in the house of God;
I trust [confidently] in the lovingkindness of God forever and ever.
AMP.9:
I will thank You forever, because You have done it, [You have rescued me and kept me safe].
I will wait on Your name, for it is good, in the presence of Your godly ones.

MSG.1:
A David psalm, when Doeg the Edomite reported to Saul,"David's at Ahimelech's house."
Why do you brag of evil, "Big Man"?
God's mercy carries the day.
MSG.2:
You scheme catastrophe;
your tongue cuts razor-sharp,
artisan in lies.
MSG.3:
You love evil more than good,
you call black white.
MSG.5:
God will tear you limb from limb,
sweep you up and throw you out,
Pull you up by the roots
from the land of life.
MSG.6:
Good people will watch and
worship. They'll laugh in relief:
MSG.7:
"Big Man bet on the wrong horse,
trusted in big money,
made his living from catastrophe."
MSG.8:
And I'm an olive tree,
growing green in God's house.
I trusted in the generous mercy
of God then and now.
MSG.9:
I thank you always
that you went into action.
And I'll stay right here,
your good name my hope,
in company with your faithful friends.

NIV.1:
Psalm 52
For the director of music. A maskil of David. When Doeg the Edomite had gone to Saul and told him: “David has gone to the house of Ahimelech.”
Why do you boast of evil, you mighty man?
Why do you boast all day long,
you who are a disgrace in the eyes of God?
NIV.2:
Your tongue plots destruction;
it is like a sharpened razor,
you who practice deceit.
NIV.3:
You love evil rather than good,
falsehood rather than speaking the truth. Selah
NIV.4:
You love every harmful word,
O you deceitful tongue!
NIV.5:
Surely God will bring you down to everlasting ruin:
He will snatch you up and tear you from your tent;
he will uproot you from the land of the living. Selah
NIV.6:
The righteous will see and fear;
they will laugh at him, saying,
NIV.7:
“Here now is the man
who did not make God his stronghold
but trusted in his great wealth
and grew strong by destroying others!”
NIV.8:
But I am like an olive tree
flourishing in the house of God;
I trust in God’s unfailing love
for ever and ever.
NIV.9:
I will praise you forever for what you have done;
in your name I will hope, for your name is good.
I will praise you in the presence of your saints.

NRSVCE.1:
Why do you boast, O mighty one,
of mischief done against the godly?
All day long
NRSVCE.2:
you are plotting destruction.
Your tongue is like a sharp razor,
you worker of treachery.
NRSVCE.3:
You love evil more than good,
and lying more than speaking the truth. Selah
NRSVCE.4:
You love all words that devour,
O deceitful tongue.
NRSVCE.5:
But God will break you down forever;
he will snatch and tear you from your tent;
he will uproot you from the land of the living. Selah
NRSVCE.6:
The righteous will see, and fear,
and will laugh at the evildoer, saying,
NRSVCE.7:
“See the one who would not take
refuge in God,
but trusted in abundant riches,
and sought refuge in wealth!”
NRSVCE.8:
But I am like a green olive tree
in the house of God.
I trust in the steadfast love of God
forever and ever.
NRSVCE.9:
I will thank you forever,
because of what you have done.
In the presence of the faithful
I will proclaim your name, for it is good.

NET.1: Why do you boast about your evil plans, O powerful man? God’s loyal love protects me all day long!
NET.2: Your tongue carries out your destructive plans; it is as effective as a sharp razor, O deceiver.
NET.3: You love evil more than good, lies more than speaking the truth. (Selah)
NET.4: You love to use all the words that destroy, and the tongue that deceives.
NET.5: Yet God will make you a permanent heap of ruins. He will scoop you up and remove you from your home; he will uproot you from the land of the living. (Selah)
NET.6: When the godly see this, they will be filled with awe, and will mock the evildoer, saying:
NET.7: “Look, here is the man who would not make God his protector! He trusted in his great wealth and was confident about his plans to destroy others.”
NET.8: But I am like a flourishing olive tree in the house of God; I continually trust in God’s loyal love.
NET.9: I will continually thank you when you execute judgment; I will rely on you, for your loyal followers know you are good.
